description: Fermi estimates for US political polarisation, expressed as single words combined into triples, plus a formula, sources and values. Based on Andris et al. (2015) network analysis across three historical phases.

words.name: partisanship
words.description: strong adherence to a political party or faction over cross-party consensus

words.name: house
words.description: in this context, the U.S. House of Representatives, the lower chamber of Congress

words.name: size
words.description: numerical extent or count of a set

words.name: early
words.description: near the beginning of a period

words.name: mid
words.description: near the middle of a period

words.name: late
words.description: near the end of a period

words.name: polarization
words.description: process by which a population splits into opposing ideological clusters

words.name: rate
words.description: frequency or proportion measured per unit of comparison

words.name: cooperation
words.description: joint action across opposing groups toward a common outcome

words.name: visual
words.description: perceived through sight, especially in graphical form

words.name: point
words.description: specific moment or value in a sequence

words.name: algorithmic
words.description: produced or driven by an algorithm, especially a computational ranking process

words.name: paranoia
words.description: condition of persistent, unfounded suspicion of others

words.name: growth
words.description: increase in size, quantity, or extent over time

words.name: factor
words.description: quantity multiplied with others to produce a result

words.name: year
words.description: calendar year used to anchor a historical phase

triples.name: house size
triples.description: total number of voting representatives in the U.S. House of Representatives

triples.name: early polarization rate
triples.description: estimated level of network or societal splitting during the creeping beginning phase (1950s-1960s)

triples.name: mid polarization rate
triples.description: estimated level of network or societal splitting during the visual turning point phase (1970s-1980s)

triples.name: late polarization rate
triples.description: estimated level of network or societal splitting during the algorithmic paranoia phase (2000s-2012)

triples.name: early cooperation rate
triples.description: fraction of non-partisan voting pairs or super-cooperators in the early post-war network

triples.name: late cooperation rate
triples.description: fraction of non-partisan voting pairs or super-cooperators remaining by the end of the study period

triples.name: creeping beginning
triples.description: initial period where over-party cooperation began its long-term continuous decline

triples.name: visual turning point
triples.description: period where network clusters began to visibly stretch and separate

triples.name: algorithmic paranoia
triples.description: modern period characterised by absolute division into two isolated ideological clusters

triples.name: polarization growth factor
triples.description: ratio of late to early polarization rate; an order-of-magnitude measure of how far polarisation has grown

triples.name: early polarization rate in creeping beginning
triples.description: baseline level of division during the early phase

triples.name: mid polarization rate in visual turning point
triples.description: elevated division during the transition towards polarised mass media

triples.name: late polarization rate in algorithmic paranoia
triples.description: maximum separation corresponding to algorithmic social media networks

triples.name: early polarization year
triples.description: representative calendar year anchoring the creeping beginning phase

triples.name: mid polarization year
triples.description: representative calendar year anchoring the visual turning point phase

triples.name: late polarization year
triples.description: representative calendar year anchoring the algorithmic paranoia phase

formulas.name: polarization growth factor formula
formulas.expression: "polarization growth factor" = "late polarization rate" / "early polarization rate"

sources.name: Andris paper
sources.description: The Rise of Partisanship and Super-Cooperators in the U.S. House of Representatives (Andris et al., 2015)

sources.name: Fermi estimate
sources.description: an order-of-magnitude figure assumed for Fermi estimation based on empirical trends
